<!-- GENERATED by bench-corpus/run-interop.sh — do not edit by hand. -->

# Phoenix `extern js` interop — boundary cost

How expensive is it to cross the `extern js` host-FFI boundary, and how
much of that is value marshalling? This is the actionable datum from the
Phase 2.5 close: it tells us whether a browser-served Phoenix program that
leans on host/DOM calls pays a per-call tax worth optimizing. Measured on
**both WASM backends** under **Node** (the always-on interop host —
[decision A](../design-decisions.md#a-host-set--gating-node-always-on-browser-gated));
the boundary is the same JS crossing in a browser). Native interop is out
of scope (the released `phoenix build` links no host shim, so there is no
CLI path to a runnable native-interop binary; the matrix covers native
*correctness*, not perf).

## Snapshot

- **Date (UTC):** 2026-06-23 — Phoenix `9052905`
- **CPU:**
- **Kernel:**
- **Host:** `node v22.12.0` (WasmGC native; the gc glue needs node >= 22)
- **Method:** `hyperfine` mean ± stddev, 2 warmup + 5 timed runs per workload (decision C minimum is 5). Each workload loops 1000000 times. Per-call cost is a *difference* of whole-process means divided by the iteration count — the subtraction cancels the fixed node-startup + glue-`instantiate` overhead the three workloads share.

## Per-call boundary cost (the signal)

Each figure is `(meanₐ − meanᵦ) / 1000000`, isolating one layer of the crossing:

| per-call cost | wasm32-linear | wasm32-gc |
|---|---|---|
| bare boundary crossing (noop − pure) | 3 ns | 2 ns |
| **String round-trip vs. pure call (string − pure)** | **837 ns** | **600 ns** |
| ↳ String marshalling only (string − noop) | 834 ns | 598 ns |

The bold row is the headline the exit criterion names — the per-call cost
of a `String` round-trip across the boundary vs. a call that never leaves
WASM. It decomposes into the bare crossing (row 1) plus the marshalling
(row 3); marshalling is where the two backends' `String` strategies differ
(linear copies via its handle table + `phx_string_alloc`; WASM-GC copies
via its scratch region).

## Whole-process times (for transparency)

These are dominated by a fixed node-startup + `instantiate` cost (tens of
ms) paid once per process — which is exactly what the per-call table above
subtracts out. They are not a per-call cost; they show the raw measurements
the deltas come from.

| workload | wasm32-linear | wasm32-gc |
|---|---|---|
| pure-WASM call (baseline, no boundary) | 35.85 ms ± 849.9 µs | 33.42 ms ± 1.22 ms |
| bare extern crossing (no marshalling) | 39.32 ms ± 1.65 ms | 35.18 ms ± 1.41 ms |
| String round-trip (out + in) | 873.23 ms ± 25.85 ms | 633.33 ms ± 4.39 ms |

## Reading the numbers

- **Per-call, not whole-program.** These are micro-costs of a single
  boundary crossing. A real program crosses the boundary a handful of
  times per frame / request, not a million times in a tight loop, so the
  boundary is rarely the bottleneck — this bench exists to *know* the cost,
  not because it is currently a problem.
- **The baseline may be inlined.** V8 can inline the intra-WASM `work` call
  in `interop_pure_call`, so that column is closer to a "calls that stay in
  WASM are ~free" floor than a true call cost. That makes the boundary
  deltas (which subtract it) a slight *over*-estimate of the crossing's
  marginal cost — the honest direction for a cost we want to bound.
- **The bare-crossing row is the noisiest.** `interop_noop_extern`'s loop
  body does one fewer add than `interop_pure_call`'s (no accumulator), so
  `noop − pure` is a low-single-digit-ns figure dominated by run-to-run
  noise — read it as "the bare crossing is nearly free," not a precise
  cost. The headline (`string − pure`) is unaffected: both loops do the
  same `acc = acc + <call>(…)` work, so only the called function differs.
- **The host stubs do nothing.** `nop` returns immediately and `echo`
  hands its argument straight back, so the measured cost is the glue +
  engine crossing + marshalling, not host work.
- **String allocation is real.** Each `String` round-trip allocates a fresh
  GC-managed Phoenix string (decision F: copied, never shared), so the
  marshalling row includes per-call allocation + GC pressure — representative
  of real string-returning host APIs.

## Reproducing locally

```sh
# Requires: cargo, node (>= 22), hyperfine on PATH.
cargo build -p phoenix-runtime --target wasm32-wasip1 --release
bash bench-corpus/run-interop.sh
git diff docs/perf/phoenix-interop-boundary.md
```
